headline: US‑Iran Deal Sparks Tension as Israel Strikes Senior Hamas Leaders
subheadline: IDF eliminated commanders Mahmad Saeed Ahmad Namaruti and Ma'awiya Salim Zakar Aidi in Gaza, while Trump warned of bombing if Iran breaches the $300 billion pact.
lede: Israel's IDF killed two senior Hamas commanders in Gaza, demonstrating precise strike capability. President Trump unveiled a U.S.–Iran agreement that would provide $300 billion for Iran's reconstruction, while threatening renewed bombing if Tehran does not comply. Iran's parliament speaker warned the Strait of Hormuz will not revert to pre‑war conditions, heightening regional tension. Meanwhile, Ukrainian UAV activity intensified across multiple fronts, with strike drones reported from the south, north and Black Sea corridors.

sections.title: Ukrainian UAV Surge Presses Russian Fronts
sections.body: Ukrainian UAVs launched from the south moved toward Veselivka in the Zaporizhzhia region. Additional strike drones were observed over the Sumy region, flying routes from Shostka‑Voronizh toward Poltava. Drones also operated from Kherson heading toward the Mykolaiv region, while guided aviation bombs were dropped in Dnipropetrovsk. Ballistic missile alerts appeared over Poltava and Kremenchuk, and a reported Ukrainian drone attack on a bus in Bryansk was denied by the Ukrainian General Staff.

sections.title: Israel Targets Hamas Leaders Amid US‑Iran Deal
sections.body: The IDF killed senior Hamas commanders Mahmad Saeed Ahmad Namaruti and Ma'awiya Salim Zakar Aidi in targeted Gaza strikes. President Trump announced a U.S.–Iran agreement that would finance $300 billion for Iran’s reconstruction and warned of bombing if Tehran fails to comply. Iran’s Parliament Speaker Qalibaf warned that the Strait of Hormuz will not return to pre‑war conditions, signaling continued strategic leverage. Images of a new U.S. military base near Gaza circulated, and alarms in Zikhron prompted an interceptor launch after a possible hostile aircraft intrusion.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: Israel’s precise elimination of Hamas leadership shows heightened strike capability, but the broader conflict remains unresolved. The U.S.–Iran financial pact combined with Trump’s bombing threats creates a volatile security environment, while Iranian assertions over Hormuz risk global energy disruptions. In Ukraine, expanding UAV operations across multiple axes increase pressure on Russian defenses, and missile alerts suggest preparation for further strikes.
